subject: Public Health
title: COVID-19 and the Mental Health Tsunami
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on COVID-19 and the Mental Health Tsunami.

hook.text: COVID-19 triggered a 25% global increase in anxiety and depression in its first year alone.

buildup.text: Lockdowns, isolation, grief, job loss, and uncertainty created a perfect storm for mental health crises.

discovery.text: Healthcare workers suffered most—burnout, moral injury, and PTSD rates skyrocketed among frontline staff.

twist.text: Telehealth exploded overnight—making therapy accessible to millions who'd never used it before.

climax.text: The pandemic proved mental health can't be an afterthought—it must be central to emergency preparedness.

punchline.text: The virus attacked lungs. The pandemic attacked minds.

quiz.question: By how much did global anxiety and depression increase in COVID-19's first year?
